sections.paragraph:
ἀχαριστέω (s. ἀχάριστος, χαρίζομαι; in lit. and ins; SyrBar 13:12 [?]; Jos., Bell. 2, 400; PGM XXII a 1, 5) be ungrateful, a cardinal sociocultural crime in the Gr-Rom. world (Antiphon Soph. 54; Demosth. 18, 119; X., Mem. 2, 2, 2 et al.) τινί toward someone (Plut., Phocyl. 758 [36, 5]; Vi. Philonidis p. 13 Crönert) τῷ θεῷ PtK 2 p. 14, 21.—DELG s.v. χάρις.

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

ins Ins, ins = Inscription, Inschrift, inscription(s). Without a period, esp. in lists, as at the beginning of entries; the capitalized form is used in titles. In conjunction with literary works this abbr. refers to the title or description of contents.

SyrBar SyrBar = Apocalypsis Syriaca Baruch—List 2

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

PGM PGM = Papyri Graecae Magicae—List 4

Gr-Rom. Gr-Rom. = Greco-Roman (gener. in contrast to Israelite/Christian tradition)

Soph Soph , V b.c.—List 5

Demosth Demosth , IV b.c.—List 5

  1. X. = Xenophon, V–IV b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

Plut Plut , I–II a.d.—List 5

Vi. Vi. = Vita, Vitae

PtK PtK = Petruskerygma (Preaching of Peter)—List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 159
